---
title: "Endocrine System"
subtitle: "Major Functions of the Endocrine System"
format:
  revealjs: 
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: 
      buttons: false
    preview-links: auto
    logo: images/quarto.png
    css: styles.css
    footer: '[https://quarto.org](https://quarto.org)'
    include-after-body: date.html
resources:
  - demo.pdf
revealjs-plugins:
  - editable
filters:
  - editable
---

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Introduction

-   The endocrine system is a network of glands that produce and release hormones.
-   Hormones regulate growth, metabolism, and reproduction.
-   Maintains internal balance (homeostasis).

**Citation:** OpenStax, *Anatomy & Physiology* (2022)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Major Endocrine Glands

-   Pituitary gland (master gland)
-   Thyroid gland
-   Adrenal glands
-   Pancreas
-   Pineal gland

**Citation:** Guyton & Hall, *Textbook of Medical Physiology* (2021)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Hormones and Their Functions

-   Hormones are chemical messengers in the bloodstream.
-   They affect target organs and tissues.

**Examples:** - Insulin → regulates blood glucose\
- Thyroxine → controls metabolism

**Citation:** OpenStax (2022)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Regulation and Feedback

-   The endocrine system uses feedback loops.
-   Negative feedback is the most common mechanism.
-   Helps maintain stable internal conditions.

**Citation:** Guyton & Hall (2021)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Conclusion

-   The endocrine system is essential for maintaining body balance.
-   Works closely with the nervous system.
-   Understanding it helps explain many diseases and body processes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## References

-   OpenStax. *Anatomy & Physiology*. 2022.
-   Guyton, A.C., & Hall, J.E. *Textbook of Medical Physiology*. 2021.
